revision.comment: 「従六位」から別項化しました
revision.text:
'''正八位'''（しょうはちい）は、[[日本]]の[[位階]]における位の一つ。[[従七位]]の下、[[従八位]]の上に位する。
[[律令制]]においては、さらに正八位上と正八位下の二階に分けられた。[[明治時代]]初期の[[太政官制]]においては上下の別がなくされた。正八位は、[[神祇官]]の権少史、[[太政官]]の[[主記]]などに相当する。

revision.text:
{{No footnotes|date=2015年8月}}
'''Shōhachi-i''' (正八位) is one of the {{ill|Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Jūshichi-i|ja|従七位}} and above {{ill|Jūhachi-i|ja|従八位}}.
In the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, Shōhachi-i was further divided into two subranks: Upper Shōhachi-i (正八位上) and Lower Shōhachi-i (正八位下). During the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制}}, these subranks were eliminated. Shōhachi-i corresponded to positions such as assistant scribes (権少史) in the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官 (明治時代)}} and chief clerks ({{ill|Shuki|ja|主記}}) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)}}.
Under the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), Shōhachi-i was the rank immediately above the lowest rank, {{ill|Jūhachi-i|ja|従八位}}, in the hierarchy of {{ill|Ranks|ja|位階}}.
== External links ==
[https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 位階令] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索}} [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - National Diet Library Digital Archive {{日本の位階}} [[Category:Rank system of Japan]]
[[ja:正八位]]

{{No footnotes|date=2015年8月}}
'''Shōhachi-i''' (正八位) is one of the {{ill|Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Jūshichi-i|ja|従七位}} and above {{ill|Jūhachi-i|ja|従八位}}.
In the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, Shōhachi-i was further divided into two subranks: Upper Shōhachi-i (正八位上) and Lower Shōhachi-i (正八位下). During the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制}}, these subranks were eliminated. Shōhachi-i corresponded to positions such as assistant scribes (権少史) in the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官 (明治時代)}} and chief clerks ({{ill|Shuki|ja|主記}}) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)}}.
Under the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), Shōhachi-i was the rank immediately above the lowest rank, {{ill|Jūhachi-i|ja|従八位}}, in the hierarchy of {{ill|Ranks|ja|位階}}.

revision.comment: fixing this to the standardized pagename
revision.text:
{{No footnotes|date=2015年8月}}
'''Shōhachi-i''' (正八位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位}} and above {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位}}.
In the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, Shōhachi-i was further divided into two subranks: Upper Shōhachi-i (正八位上) and Lower Shōhachi-i (正八位下). During the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制}}, these subranks were eliminated. Shōhachi-i corresponded to positions such as assistant scribes (権少史) in the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官 (明治時代)}} and chief clerks ({{ill|Shuki|ja|主記}}) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)}}.
Under the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), Shōhachi-i was the rank immediately above the lowest rank, {{ill|Jūhachi-i|ja|従八位}}, in the hierarchy of {{ill|Ranks|ja|位階}}.

Shōhachi-i corresponded to positions such as assistant scribes (権少史) in the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官 (明治時代)}} and chief clerks ({{ill|Shuki|ja|主記}}) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)}}.
Under the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), Shōhachi-i was the rank immediately above the lowest rank, {{ill|Jūhachi-i|ja|従八位}}, in the hierarchy of {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}.

During the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制}}, these subranks were eliminated. Shōhachi-i corresponded to positions such as assistant scribes (権少史) in the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官 (明治時代)}} and chief clerks ({{ill|Shuki|ja|主記}}) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)}}.
Under the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), Shōhachi-i was the rank immediately above the lowest rank, {{ill|Jūhachi-i|ja|従八位}}, in the hierarchy of {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}.

revision.text:
{{No footnotes|date=2015年8月}}
'''Shōhachi-i''' (正八位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位}} and above {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位}}.
In the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, Shōhachi-i was further divided into two subranks: Upper Shōhachi-i (正八位上) and Lower Shōhachi-i (正八位下). During the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制}}, these subranks were eliminated. Shōhachi-i corresponded to positions such as assistant scribes (権少史) in the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官 (明治時代)}} and chief clerks ({{ill|Shuki|ja|主記}}) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)}}.
Under the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), Shōhachi-i was the rank immediately above the lowest rank, {{ill|Jūhachi-i|ja|従八位}}, in the hierarchy of {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}.

revision.text:
{{No footnotes|date=2015年8月}}
'''Shōhachi-i''' (正八位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位}} and above {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位}}.
In the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, Shōhachi-i was further divided into two subranks: Upper Shōhachi-i (正八位上) and Lower Shōhachi-i (正八位下). During the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制}}, these subranks were eliminated. Shōhachi-i corresponded to positions such as assistant scribes (権少史) in the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官 (明治時代)}} and chief clerks ({{ill|Shuki|ja|主記}}) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)}}.
Under the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), Shōhachi-i was the rank immediately above the lowest rank, {{ill|Jūhachi-i|ja|従八位}}, in the hierarchy of {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}.
== External links ==
[https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 位階令] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索}} [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - National Diet Library Digital Archive [[Category:Rank system of Japan]] [[Category:Court Ranks of Japan]] [[Category:Senior Eighth Rank]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Senior Eighth Rank-qq]] [[Category:Senior Eighth Rank]][[Category:Court Ranks of Japan]] [[Category:Senior Eighth Rank]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Senior Eighth Rank-qq]] [[Category:Senior Eighth Rank]]{{Court Ranks of Japan}} * <!--ranklist-->{{ill|Senior First Rank|ja|正一位}} {{ill|Junior First Rank|ja|従一位}} {{ill|Senior Second Rank|ja|正二位}} {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位}} {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位}} {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位}} {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank|ja|正四位}} {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位}} {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|正五位}} {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位}} {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位}} {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位}} {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位}} {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位}} {{ill|Senior Eighth Rank|ja|正八位}} {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位}} {{ill|Senior Ninth Rank|ja|正九位}} {{ill|Junior Ninth Rank|ja|従九位}} {{ill|Great Initial Rank|ja|大初位}} {{ill|Minor Initial Rank|ja|少初位}}<!--endranklist-->[[Category:Court Ranks of Japan]] [[Category:Senior Eighth Rank]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Senior Eighth Rank-qq]] [[Category:Senior Eighth Rank]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:正八位]]

revision.text:
{{No footnotes|date=2015年8月}}
'''Shōhachi-i''' (正八位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位}} and above {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位}}.
In the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, Shōhachi-i was further divided into two subranks: Upper Shōhachi-i (正八位上) and Lower Shōhachi-i (正八位下). During the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制}}, these subranks were eliminated. Shōhachi-i corresponded to positions such as assistant scribes (権少史) in the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官 (明治時代)}} and chief clerks ({{ill|Shuki|ja|主記}}) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)}}.
Under the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), Shōhachi-i was the rank immediately above the lowest rank, {{ill|Jūhachi-i|ja|従八位}}, in the hierarchy of {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}.
== External links ==

{{No footnotes|date=2015年8月}}
'''Shōhachi-i''' (正八位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位}} and above {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位}}.
In the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, Shōhachi-i was further divided into two subranks: Upper Shōhachi-i (正八位上) and Lower Shōhachi-i (正八位下). During the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制}}, these subranks were eliminated. Shōhachi-i corresponded to positions such as assistant scribes (権少史) in the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官 (明治時代)}} and chief clerks ({{ill|Shuki|ja|主記}}) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)}}.
Under the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), Shōhachi-i was the rank immediately above the lowest rank, {{ill|Jūhachi-i|ja|従八位}}, in the hierarchy of {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}.

revision.text:
[[Category:new pages]] {{No footnotes|date=2015年8月}}
'''Shōhachi-i''' (正八位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位}} and above {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位}}.
In the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, Shōhachi-i was further divided into two subranks: Upper Shōhachi-i (正八位上) and Lower Shōhachi-i (正八位下). During the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制}}, these subranks were eliminated. Shōhachi-i corresponded to positions such as assistant scribes (権少史) in the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官 (明治時代)}} and chief clerks ({{ill|Shuki|ja|主記}}) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)}}.
Under the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), Shōhachi-i was the rank immediately above the lowest rank, {{ill|Jūhachi-i|ja|従八位}}, in the hierarchy of {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}.

During the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制}}, these subranks were eliminated. Shōhachi-i corresponded to positions such as assistant scribes (権少史) in the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官 (明治時代)}} and chief clerks ({{ill|Shuki|ja|主記}}) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)}}.
Under the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), Shōhachi-i was the rank immediately above the lowest rank, {{ill|Jūhachi-i|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank}}, in the hierarchy of {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "正四位}}" to "正四位|1=Senior Fourth Rank}}"
revision.text:
[[Category:new pages]] {{No footnotes|date=2015年8月}}
'''Shōhachi-i''' (正八位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank}} and above {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank}}.
In the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, Shōhachi-i was further divided into two subranks: Upper Shōhachi-i (正八位上) and Lower Shōhachi-i (正八位下). During the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制}}, these subranks were eliminated. Shōhachi-i corresponded to positions such as assistant scribes (権少史) in the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官 (明治時代)}} and chief clerks ({{ill|Shuki|ja|主記}}) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)}}.
Under the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), Shōhachi-i was the rank immediately above the lowest rank, {{ill|Jūhachi-i|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank}}, in the hierarchy of {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}.
== External links ==
[https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 位階令] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索}} [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - National Diet Library Digital Archive [[Category:Rank system of revision.comment: Text replacement - "従一位}}" to "従一位|1=Junior First Rank}}"
revision.text:
[[Category:new pages]] {{No footnotes|date=2015年8月}}
'''Shōhachi-i''' (正八位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank}} and above {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank}}.
In the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, Shōhachi-i was further divided into two subranks: Upper Shōhachi-i (正八位上) and Lower Shōhachi-i (正八位下). During the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制}}, these subranks were eliminated. Shōhachi-i corresponded to positions such as assistant scribes (権少史) in the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官 (明治時代)}} and chief clerks ({{ill|Shuki|ja|主記}}) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)}}.
Under the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), Shōhachi-i was the rank immediately above the lowest rank, {{ill|Jūhachi-i|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank}}, in the hierarchy of {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}.
== External links ==
[https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 位階令] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索}} [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - National Diet Library Digital Archive [[Category:Rank system of revision.comment: removing bad jawiki links
revision.text:
[[Category:new pages]] {{No footnotes|date=2015年8月}}
'''Shōhachi-i''' (正八位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank}} and above {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank}}.
In the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, Shōhachi-i was further divided into two subranks: Upper Shōhachi-i (正八位上) and Lower Shōhachi-i (正八位下). During the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制}}, these subranks were eliminated. Shōhachi-i corresponded to positions such as assistant scribes (権少史) in the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官 (明治時代)}} and chief clerks ({{ill|Shuki|ja|主記}}) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)}}.
Under the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), Shōhachi-i was the rank immediately above the lowest rank, {{ill|Jūhachi-i|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank}}, in the hierarchy of {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}.
== External links ==
[https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 位階令] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索}} [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - National Diet Library Digital Archive [[Category:Rank system of revision.text:
[[Category:new pages]] {{No footnotes|date=2015年8月}}
'''Shōhachi-i''' (正八位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank}} and above {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank}}.
In the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, Shōhachi-i was further divided into two subranks: Upper Shōhachi-i (正八位上) and Lower Shōhachi-i (正八位下). During the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制}}, these subranks were eliminated. Shōhachi-i corresponded to positions such as assistant scribes (権少史) in the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官 (明治時代)}} and chief clerks ({{ill|Shuki|ja|主記}}) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)}}.
Under the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), Shōhachi-i was the rank immediately above the lowest rank, {{ill|Jūhachi-i|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank}}, in the hierarchy of {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}.

Under the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), Shōhachi-i was the rank immediately above the lowest rank, {{ill|Jūhachi-i|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank}}, in the hierarchy of {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}.

revision.text:
[[Category:Completion tier 2]] {{No footnotes|date=2015年8月}}
'''Shōhachi-i''' (正八位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank}} and above {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank}}.
In the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, Shōhachi-i was further divided into two subranks: Upper Shōhachi-i (正八位上) and Lower Shōhachi-i (正八位下). During the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制}}, these subranks were eliminated. Shōhachi-i corresponded to positions such as assistant scribes (権少史) in the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|blank= (明治時代)}} and chief clerks ({{ill|Shuki|ja|主記}}) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)}}.
Under the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), Shōhachi-i was the rank immediately above the lowest rank, {{ill|Jūhachi-i|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank}}, in the hierarchy of {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}.
== External links ==
[https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 位階令] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索}} [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - National Diet Library Digital Archive [[Category:Rank system of [[Category:Completion tier 3]] {{No footnotes|date=2015年8月}}
'''Shōhachi-i''' (正八位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank}} and above {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank}}.
In the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, Shōhachi-i was further divided into two subranks: Upper Shōhachi-i (正八位上) and Lower Shōhachi-i (正八位下). During the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制}}, these subranks were eliminated. Shōhachi-i corresponded to positions such as assistant scribes (権少史) in the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|blank= (明治時代)}} and chief clerks ({{ill|Shuki|ja|主記}}) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)}}.
Under the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), Shōhachi-i was the rank immediately above the lowest rank, {{ill|Jūhachi-i|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank}}, in the hierarchy of {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}.
== External links ==

revision.comment: re-adding the categories that I accidentally got rid of
revision.text:
[[Category:Court Ranks of Japan]][[Category:completion tier 4]] {{No footnotes|date=2015年8月}}
'''Shōhachi-i''' (正八位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank}} and above {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank}}.
In the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, Shōhachi-i was further divided into two subranks: Upper Shōhachi-i (正八位上) and Lower Shōhachi-i (正八位下). During the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制}}, these subranks were eliminated. Shōhachi-i corresponded to positions such as assistant scribes (権少史) in the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|blank= (明治時代)}} and chief clerks ({{ill|Shuki|ja|主記}}) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)}}.
Under the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), Shōhachi-i was the rank immediately above the lowest rank, {{ill|Jūhachi-i|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank}}, in the hierarchy of {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}.
== External links ==
[https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 位階令] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索}} [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - National Diet Library Digital Archive [[Category:Court Ranks of Japan]][[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Court Ranks of Japan]][[Category:completion tier 4]]

[[Category:Court Ranks of Japan]][[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]] {{No footnotes|date=2015th year 8月}}
'''Shōhachi-i''' (正八位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank}} and above {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-ranks/court-ranks/</ref>
In the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, Shōhachi-i was further divided into two subranks: Upper Shōhachi-i (正八位上) and Lower Shōhachi-i (正八位下). During the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制}}, these subranks were eliminated. Shōhachi-i corresponded to positions such as assistant scribes (権少史) in the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|blank= (明治時代)}} and chief clerks ({{ill|Shuki|ja|主記}}) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)}}.
Under the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), Shōhachi-i was the rank immediately above the lowest rank, {{ill|Jūhachi-i|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank}}, in the hierarchy of {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}.

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Original templates]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{No footnotes|date=2015th year 8月}}
'''Shōhachi-i''' (正八位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階|12=draft}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|12=draft}} and above {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank|12=draft}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-ranks/court-ranks/</ref>
In the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|12=draft}}, Shōhachi-i was further divided into two subranks: Upper Shōhachi-i (正八位上) and Lower Shōhachi-i (正八位下). During the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|12=draft}}, under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制|12=draft}}, these subranks were eliminated. Shōhachi-i corresponded to positions such as assistant scribes (権少史) in the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|blank= (明治時代)|12=draft}} and chief clerks ({{ill|Shuki|ja|主記|12=draft}}) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)|12=draft}}.
Under the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例|12=draft}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令|12=draft}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), Shōhachi-i was the rank immediately above the lowest rank, {{ill|Jūhachi-i|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank|12=draft}}, in the hierarchy of {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階|12=draft}}.
== External links ==
[https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 位階令] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索|12=draft}} [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - National Diet Library Digital Archive <!--Senior Eighth Rank--><!--Senior Eighth Rank-->{{Court Ranks of Japan}} * <!--ranklist: {{ill|Senior First Rank|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|12=draft}} {{ill|Junior First Rank|ja|従一位|1=Junior First Rank|12=draft}} {{ill|Senior Second Rank|ja|正二位|1=Senior Second Rank|12=draft}} {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank|12=draft}} {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=draft}} {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=draft}} {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank|ja|正四位|1=Senior Fourth Rank|12=draft}} {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=draft}} {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|正五位|1=Senior Fifth Rank|12=draft}} {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|1=Junior Fifth Rank|12=draft}} {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位|1=Senior Sixth Rank|12=draft}} {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|12=draft}} {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位|1=Senior Seventh Rank|12=draft}} {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|12=draft}} {{ill|Senior Eighth Rank|ja|正八位|1=Senior Eighth Rank|12=draft}} {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank|12=draft}} {{ill|Senior Ninth Rank|ja|正九位|1=Senior Ninth Rank|12=draft}} {{ill|Junior Ninth Rank|ja|従九位|1=Junior Ninth Rank|12=draft}} {{ill|Greater Initial Rank|ja|大初位|1=Greater Initial Rank|12=draft}} {{ill|Lesser Initial Rank|ja|少初位|1=Lesser Initial Rank|12=draft}} endranklist--><!--Senior Eighth Rank-->

'''Shōhachi-i''' (正八位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Court Ranks of Japan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|qq=}} and above {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>

In the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], Shōhachi-i was further divided into two subRanks: Upper Shōhachi-i (正八位上) and Lower Shōhachi-i (正八位下). During the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太政官 (明治時代)|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}}, these subRanks were eliminated. Shōhachi-i corresponded to positions such as assistant scribes (権少史) in the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] and chief clerks ({{ill|Shuki|ja|主記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan|qq=|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}}.

Under the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joi Jōrei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikai-ryō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), Shōhachi-i was the Rank immediately above the lowest Rank, {{ill|Jūhachi-i|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūhachi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}}, in the hierarchy of {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Court Ranks of Japan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:正八位]][[ko:정8위]][[zh:正八位]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11545368]]
Wikidata name: Senior Eighth Rank
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{No footnotes|date=2015th year 8月}}

'''Shōhachi-i''' (正八位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Court Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|qq=}} and above {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>

In the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], Shōhachi-i was further divided into two subRanks: Upper Shōhachi-i (正八位上) and Lower Shōhachi-i (正八位下). During the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太政官 (明治時代)|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}}, these subRanks were eliminated. Shōhachi-i corresponded to positions such as assistant scribes (権少史) in the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] and chief clerks ({{ill|Shuki|ja|主記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}}.

Under the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joi Jōrei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikai-ryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), Shōhachi-i was the Rank immediately above the lowest Rank, {{ill|Jūhachi-i|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūhachi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}}, in the hierarchy of {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Court Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:正八位]][[ko:정8위]][[zh:正八位]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11545368]]
Wikidata name: Senior Eighth Rank
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{No footnotes|date=2015th year 8月}}

'''Shōhachi-i''' (正八位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Court Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|qq=}} and above {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>

In the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], Shōhachi-i was further divided into two subRanks: Upper Shōhachi-i (正八位上) and Lower Shōhachi-i (正八位下). During the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太政官 (明治時代)|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}}, these subRanks were eliminated. Shōhachi-i corresponded to positions such as assistant scribes (権少史) in the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] and chief clerks ({{ill|Shuki|ja|主記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}}.

Under the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joi Jōrei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikai-ryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), Shōhachi-i was the Rank immediately above the lowest Rank, {{ill|Jūhachi-i|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūhachi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}}, in the hierarchy of {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Court Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.

'''Shōhachi-i''' (正八位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Court Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|qq=}} and above {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>

In the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], Shōhachi-i was further divided into two subRanks: Upper Shōhachi-i (正八位上) and Lower Shōhachi-i (正八位下). During the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太政官 (明治時代)|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}}, these subRanks were eliminated. Shōhachi-i corresponded to positions such as assistant scribes (権少史) in the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] and chief clerks ({{ill|Shuki|ja|主記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}}.

Under the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joi Jōrei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikai-ryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), Shōhachi-i was the Rank immediately above the lowest Rank, {{ill|Jūhachi-i|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūhachi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}}, in the hierarchy of {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Court Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.

revision.comment: Bot: add interwikis from Wikidata
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:正八位]][[ko:정8위]][[zh:正八位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:正八位]][[ko:정8위]][[zh:正八位]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11545368]] Wikidata name: Senior Eighth Rank [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{No footnotes|date=2015th year 8月}}
'''Shōhachi-i''' (正八位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Court Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|qq=}} and above {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>
In the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], Shōhachi-i was further divided into two subRanks: Upper Shōhachi-i (正八位上) and Lower Shōhachi-i (正八位下). During the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太政官 (明治時代)|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}}, these subRanks were eliminated. Shōhachi-i corresponded to positions such as assistant scribes (権少史) in the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] and chief clerks ({{ill|Shuki|ja|主記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}}.
Under the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joi Jōrei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikai-ryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), Shōhachi-i was the Rank immediately above the lowest Rank, {{ill|Jūhachi-i|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūhachi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}}, in the hierarchy of {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Court Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵↵↵↵" to " "
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:正八位]][[ko:정8위]][[zh:正八位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:正八位]][[ko:정8위]][[zh:正八位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:正八位]][[ko:정8위]][[zh:正八位]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11545368]] Wikidata name: Senior Eighth Rank [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{No footnotes|date=2015th year 8月}}
'''Shōhachi-i''' (正八位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Court Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|qq=}} and above {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>
In the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], Shōhachi-i was further divided into two subRanks: Upper Shōhachi-i (正八位上) and Lower Shōhachi-i (正八位下). During the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太政官 (明治時代)|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}}, these subRanks were eliminated. Shōhachi-i corresponded to positions such as assistant scribes (権少史) in the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] and chief clerks ({{ill|Shuki|ja|主記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}}.
Under the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joi Jōrei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikai-ryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), Shōhachi-i was the Rank immediately above the lowest Rank, {{ill|Jūhachi-i|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūhachi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}}, in the hierarchy of {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Court Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.

[https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 位階令] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/e-Gov Law Search|qq=}} [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - National Diet Library Digital Archive {{Court Ranks of Japan}} *

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:正八位]][[ko:정8위]][[zh:正八位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:正八位]][[ko:정8위]][[zh:正八位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:正八位]][[ko:정8위]][[zh:正八位]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11545368]] Wikidata name: Senior Eighth Rank [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{No footnotes|date=2015th year 8月}}
'''Shōhachi-i''' (正八位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Court Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|qq=}} and above {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>
In the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system}}, Shōhachi-i was further divided into two subRanks: Upper Shōhachi-i (正八位上) and Lower Shōhachi-i (正八位下). During the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}, under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太政官 (明治時代)|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}}, these subRanks were eliminated. Shōhachi-i corresponded to positions such as assistant scribes (権少史) in the {{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|WD=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities}} and chief clerks ({{ill|Shuki|ja|主記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}}.
Under the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joi Jōrei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikai-ryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), Shōhachi-i was the Rank immediately above the lowest Rank, {{ill|Jūhachi-i|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūhachi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}}, in the hierarchy of {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Court Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.

'''Shōhachi-i''' (正八位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Court Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|qq=}} and above {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>
In the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system}}, Shōhachi-i was further divided into two subRanks: Upper Shōhachi-i (正八位上) and Lower Shōhachi-i (正八位下). During the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}, under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太政官 (明治時代)|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}}, these subRanks were eliminated. '''Shōhachi-i''' (正八位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Court Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|qq=}} and above {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>
In the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system}}, Shōhachi-i was further divided into two subRanks: Upper Shōhachi-i (正八位上) and Lower Shōhachi-i (正八位下). During the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}, under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太政官 (明治時代)|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}}, these subRanks were eliminated. Shōhachi-i corresponded to positions such as assistant scribes (権少史) in the {{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|WD=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities}} and chief clerks ({{ill|Shuki|ja|主記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}}.
Under the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joi Jōrei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikai-ryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), Shōhachi-i was the Rank immediately above the lowest Rank, {{ill|Jūhachi-i|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūhachi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}}, in the hierarchy of {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Court Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.

Under the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joi Jōrei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikai-ryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=Court Ranks in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), Shōhachi-i was the Rank immediately above the lowest Rank, {{ill|Jūhachi-i|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūhachi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}}, in the hierarchy of {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Court Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.
